behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-API-client handoff (request builder + transport call), not model quality. A pure unit test of a helper can miss dropped fields during wiring. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (stub client is enough), no AI output dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about request payload inclusion, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake in-process provider client that captures outbound payload; call classify with a taxonomy and assert the outbound request contains every allowed label exactly once before send.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at response handoff boundary (provider response -> normalization -> public return), where wiring can bypass validation. Integration-brake: can be reproduced with in-process fake response; no real OS/network needed, no dependence on model judgment, no multi-step agent chain. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on invalid-label sanitization logic, not live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider returning an out-of-taxonomy label; invoke classify end-to-end within process and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is a pure determinism property of normalization logic for identical inputs. No module boundary, I/O, or external dependency is required to expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical output label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI provider behavior across live calls; stubs cannot reveal model-side variability or drift. This is single-step repeated classification, not a multi-agent pipeline. Rejected Workflow: no chained agentic stages are described.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real-provider classifications for fixed document+taxonomy, log provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes occur only when raw provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is in interaction between token-budget logic and dispatch path (must prevent or modify send before provider call). A unit test of tokenizer alone can miss accidental dispatch. Integration-brake: in-process fake provider is sufficient; no real OS/network required; no model-output dependence; no multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low budget and use an over-budget document with fake provider client; assert policy action (truncate or reject) occurs and outbound request obeys budget, including no dispatch when policy is reject.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI API call and real provider usage accounting; mocks can hide tokenizer/version/provider-side differences. Even with deterministic assertion, execution depends on live AI API behavior/metadata. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic call path is required, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents against the real provider and assert provider-reported token usage per call is <= configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across local modules (error mapping, backoff/wait policy, retry loop). This can be caught with fake provider and controllable clock/timer. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is necessary if retry-after signal is simulated; no dependence on model output quality; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that first returns rate-limit response then success; run classify with fake clock and assert the module waits/backs off, retries expected times, and eventually returns success instead of immediate error.
